enraged: A surge of inexplicable rage floods through you. Everything feels like a deliberate provocation.
Your teeth clench. Someone moved something. Someone always moves something.
A flash of fury — white-hot and sourceless — burns through your chest.
The island itself feels like it's mocking you. Every sound, every shadow.
glad: A wave of warmth washes over you. Everything is exactly as it should be.
You feel a deep contentment radiating outward from somewhere behind your ribs.
The island is beautiful. You are grateful to be here. Everything is fine.
A glow of quiet satisfaction settles over you like morning light.
anxious: Something is watching you from the treeline. You can't see it. But it's there.
You hear a sound that wasn't there a moment ago. Or was it? You're not sure.
The shadows look wrong. Something is moving in the periphery and it stops when you turn.
Your heart is hammering. There's no reason. That makes it worse.
A voice — very faint, very close — seems to whisper your name.
The ground feels unsteady. Like something underneath is breathing.
confident: You feel invincible. Nothing on this island is a threat to you.
A surge of certainty flows through you — you know exactly what needs to be done.
You could build anything right now. You could face anything. You are ready.
Every decision you've made has been correct. You are sure of this.
sad: A profound sadness settles over you, sourceless and heavy.
You miss somewhere you've never been. The feeling is unbearable.
Something precious has been lost. You can't remember what. That makes it worse.
The colors look duller than before. The island feels very far from home.
happy: A burst of pure joy rises in your chest for no reason at all.
Everything feels lighter than usual. The air itself seems warmer.
You feel a sudden urge to laugh. You're not sure why. That's fine.
The island is beautiful. You are exactly where you're supposed to be.
Something wonderful is about to happen. You can feel it.
